nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock was born on 14 September 1377, in Woodford, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir William de Clinton, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Deincourt, was 15. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Lady Anne Botreaux.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock est né le 14 septembre 1377, à Woodford, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Clinton, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Deincourt, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Lady Anne Botreaux.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock am 14. September 1377 in Woodford, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Clinton 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Deincourt 15. Er hatte mit Lady Anne Botreaux mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock è nato il il 14 settembre 1377 nel Woodford, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William de Clinton, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Deincourt, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Lady Anne Botreaux.

ko.lifeSummary: William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock(이)가 1377년 9월 14일 영국 체실르 우드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Clinton(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Deincourt(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Lady Anne Botreaux(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock nasceu em 14 de setembro de 1377, em Woodford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Clinton, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Deincourt, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Lady Anne Botreaux.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock родился 14 сентября 1377 г. в местности вудфорд, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Clinton было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Deincourt, 15. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Lady Anne Botreaux.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Clinton 4th Lord of Maxtock nació el 14 de septiembre de 1377, en Woodford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Clinton, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Deincourt, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Lady Anne Botreaux.
